SEARCH
Package org.fluentjdbc

Class DatabaseSelectBuilder

java.lang.Object
org.fluentjdbc.DatabaseSelectBuilder
All Implemented Interfaces:
DatabaseListableQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>, DatabaseQueryable<DatabaseSelectBuilder>, DatabaseQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>
public class DatabaseSelectBuilder extends Object implements DatabaseQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>, DatabaseListableQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>
Used to construct SQL SELECT statements in a flexible way with DatabaseQueryable.where(String, Object) clauses, select(String...) column names, from(String) table statement, groupBy(String...), orderBy(String) and skipAndLimit(int, int).

Example:

 new DatabaseSqlBuilder()
      .select("city, avg(age) as average_age")
      .from("person")
      .groupBy("city")
      .order("avg(age) desc")
      .limit(1)
      .singleString(connection, "city");

    Executes the SELECT * FROM ... statement and calls back to DatabaseResult.RowConsumer for each returned row

    singleObject(Connection connection, DatabaseResult.RowMapper<OBJECT> mapper)
    If the query returns no rows, returns SingleRow.absent(java.util.function.Supplier<EX>), if exactly one row is returned, maps it and return it, if more than one is returned, throws `IllegalStateException`

    Execute the query and map each return value over the DatabaseResult.RowMapper function to return a stream.

    unordered()
    If you haven't called orderBy(java.lang.String), the results of DatabaseListableQueryBuilder.list(java.sql.Connection, org.fluentjdbc.DatabaseResult.RowMapper<OBJECT>) will be unpredictable.

  • Method Details

    • select

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder select(String... columns)
      Add the arguments to the column list for the SELECT column, column... statement

    • from

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder from(String fromStatement)
      Replace the "from" part of the SELECT ... FROM fromStatement in the select statement

    • where

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder where(DatabaseQueryParameter parameter)
      Adds the parameter to the WHERE-clause and all the parameter list. E.g. where(new DatabaseQueryParameter("created_at between ? and ?", List.of(earliestDate, latestDate)))
      Specified by:
      where in interface DatabaseQueryable<DatabaseSelectBuilder>

    • groupBy

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder groupBy(String... groupByStatement)
      Add the arguments to the column list for the SELECT column, column... statement

    • orderBy

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder orderBy(String orderByClause)
      Adds ORDER BY ... clause to the SELECT statement
      Specified by:
      orderBy in interface DatabaseListableQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>
      Specified by:
      orderBy in interface DatabaseQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>

    • orderBy

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder orderBy(List<String> orderByClauses)
      Sets the ORDER BY ... clause of the SELECT statement
      Specified by:
      orderBy in interface DatabaseListableQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>

    • unordered

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder unordered()
      If you haven't called orderBy(java.lang.String), the results of DatabaseListableQueryBuilder.list(java.sql.Connection, org.fluentjdbc.DatabaseResult.RowMapper<OBJECT>) will be unpredictable. Call unordered() if you are okay with this.
      Specified by:
      unordered in interface DatabaseQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>

    • skipAndLimit

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder skipAndLimit(int offset, int rowCount)
      Adds OFFSET ... ROWS FETCH ... ROWS ONLY clause to the SELECT statement. FETCH FIRST was introduced in SQL:2008 and is supported by Postgresql 8.4, Oracle 12c, IBM DB2, HSQLDB, H2, and SQL Server 2012.

    • singleObject

      @Nonnull public <OBJECT> SingleRow<OBJECT> singleObject(Connection connection, DatabaseResult.RowMapper<OBJECT> mapper)
      If the query returns no rows, returns SingleRow.absent(java.util.function.Supplier<EX>), if exactly one row is returned, maps it and return it, if more than one is returned, throws `IllegalStateException`
      Specified by:
      singleObject in interface DatabaseListableQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>
      Specified by:
      singleObject in interface DatabaseQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>
      Parameters:
      connection - Database connection
      mapper - Function object to map a single returned row to an object
      Returns:
      the mapped row if one row is returned, SingleRow.absent(java.util.function.Supplier<EX>) otherwise
      Throws:
      MultipleRowsReturnedException - if more than one row was matched the query

    • stream

      public <OBJECT> Stream<OBJECT> stream(@Nonnull Connection connection, DatabaseResult.RowMapper<OBJECT> mapper)
      Execute the query and map each return value over the DatabaseResult.RowMapper function to return a stream. Example: 
           table.where("status", status).stream(row -> row.getInstant("created_at"))
      Specified by:
      stream in interface DatabaseListableQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>

    • forEach

      public void forEach(Connection connection, DatabaseResult.RowConsumer consumer)
      Executes the SELECT * FROM ... statement and calls back to DatabaseResult.RowConsumer for each returned row
      Specified by:
      forEach in interface DatabaseListableQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>

    • getCount

      public int getCount(Connection connection)
      Executes SELECT count(*) FROM ... on the query and returns the result
      Specified by:
      getCount in interface DatabaseListableQueryBuilder<DatabaseSelectBuilder>

    • query

      public DatabaseSelectBuilder query()
      Implemented as return this for compatibility purposes
      Specified by:
      query in interface DatabaseQueryable<DatabaseSelectBuilder>

      Returns a DatabaseQueryParameter with a column in (SELECT ....) with this expression and the same parameters as this builder
